topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged, bright and intrusive, right in the middle of my study session. I squinted at my phone, a slow, deliberate movement. It was a post from Mr. Harrison. A picture of a slightly blurry landscape, captioned with a philosophical quote. My jaw tightened. I felt a distinct clenching in my chest, a sudden, unwelcome tension that made my shoulders rise toward my ears. I quickly minimized the app, shoving the device face down on the desk. I needed silence. I picked up my pen, but the ink felt sluggish, the words refusing to flow. It was just… irritating.

[story 2]

I was walking out of the library, juggling my textbooks and a lukewarm coffee, when I saw it—a notification banner glowing on my screen. It was Professor Eldridge. A photo of a rare book he’d been discussing in class, tagged with a location I recognized as the university quad. A low, simmering heat started behind my eyes. I stopped dead in my tracks, forcing myself to breathe evenly. I stared at the screen for a full minute, my knuckles whitening around my backpack straps. I shook my head, a small, sharp movement, as if trying to dislodge something unwelcome. This felt like an unwarranted intrusion into my personal space.

[story 3]

It happened during my lunch break. I was scrolling through campus news when I spotted it: a recent story about the history department, and there, in the comments section, was a comment from Mr. Davies. He had tagged me in a photo of a seminar he was leading. A wave of exasperation washed over me, making my stomach feel tight and restless. I sighed, a long, audible release of air. I scrolled past the post with aggressive speed, my thumb flicking across the screen with unnecessary force. I wanted to erase the entire interaction, to scrub the memory of his presence from my feed.
